metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: structured output must be a JSON object, got list

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command (e.g. `python -m rebar.llm.evals.eval_runner --spec eval_specs/code_review/` or equivalent) to each AC so an executor can produce deterministic evidence of completion.
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is specified for any acceptance criterion — an executor cannot deterministically verify AC1 (new run_case arm executes correctly), AC2 (scorer registered and scoring correctly), or AC5 (nit-framing cases pass on current gate) without guessing what command to run.
findings.suggested_fix: Append a 'Verification' subsection listing: (1) the harness invocation command for the new code-review arm, (2) expected output format (e.g. JSON with findings=[]), (3) the specific eval run command that must produce a passing score for AC5's nit-framing fixture.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'tests overlay' and 'security overlay' prompt IDs explicitly (e.g. `code-review-overlay-tests`, `code-review-overlay-security`) and describe the nit-framing experiment (what it tests, fixture shape, expected assertion) directly in this plan or via a linked reference.
findings.finding: 'tests + security overlays' (AC3) and 'nit-framing experiment' (AC5) are referenced as known concepts without being defined or cross-referenced in this plan; an executor who lacks broader epic context cannot identify which overlay prompt IDs or fixture files these map to.
findings.location: AC3 ('at least the tests + security overlays'), AC5 ('the nit-framing experiment is encoded')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Definitions' subsection listing: overlay prompt IDs and their file locations, plus a one-paragraph description of the nit-framing experiment (the defect it exercises, the no-fire and recall expectations).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between Scope ('No gate behavior change') and What (conditional _fired/FIRE_EXPECTS extension): either tighten Scope to 'No gate-verdict behavior change' (clarifying that scorer internals may expand) or explicitly call out this exception.
findings.finding: The 'Scope' section declares 'No gate behavior change,' but the 'What' section describes a conditional extension of eval_scorers._fired() to also match verdict=='BLOCK' and adding 'block' to FIRE_EXPECTS — both of which are behavioral changes to existing gate/scorer infrastructure, directly contradicting the no-gate-change constraint.
findings.location: Scope section ('No gate behavior change') vs. What section (OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT paragraph: 'extend _fired to also match verdict==\'BLOCK\' and add \'block\' to FIRE_EXPECTS')
findings.suggested_fix: Change Scope to: 'No change to gate-verdict behavior. Scorer internals (_fired, FIRE_EXPECTS) may expand only as needed to support new case types without altering existing FAIL-verdict logic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description of the nit-framing experiment (what it is, what fixture it requires, what scorer assertion it encodes) to the 'What' section so AC #5 maps to described work.
findings.finding: AC #5 references 'the nit-framing experiment' as if it is a known, described artifact ('its no-fire/recall expectations pass on the current gate as baseline'), but this experiment is never introduced, defined, or described anywhere in the plan body (Why, What, or Scope). This makes AC #5 an orphan criterion measuring undescribed work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC #5: 'The nit-framing experiment is encoded as a committed eval case...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion covering the conditional _fired/FIRE_EXPECTS extension (or explicitly state it is out of scope and remove it from 'What' to eliminate the orphan work).
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes a concrete deliverable — the conditional extension of eval_scorers._fired() to match verdict=='BLOCK' and the addition of 'block' to FIRE_EXPECTS — but no acceptance criterion covers or verifies this work. It is described work with no covering AC.
findings.location: What section, OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT paragraph vs. Acceptance Criteria (ACs #1–#5)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'If any eval case exercises the full-gate verdict path, eval_scorers._fired() matches verdict=="BLOCK" and FIRE_EXPECTS includes "block"; existing FAIL-verdict cases are unaffected.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit expand-contract note for the `_fired()` / `FIRE_EXPECTS` change: audit existing cases for BLOCK verdicts, document the before/after scoring impact, and specify a rollback path (e.g., a feature flag or a separate `_fired_gate()` helper that leaves the original `_fired()` untouched).
findings.finding: The plan conditionally extends `_fired()` to match `verdict=='BLOCK'` and adds 'block' to `FIRE_EXPECTS`, modifying existing scorer behavior for all callers, but provides no rollback path or expand-contract sequence if existing full-gate cases are inadvertently affected.
findings.suggested_fix: Instead of mutating the shared `_fired()`, introduce a new `_fired_gate()` that matches FAIL|BLOCK and call it only from full-gate scorers. Leave the existing `_fired()` unchanged, eliminating any compat risk entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the two-pipeline scoring architecture (findings-based for code-review vs. verdict-based for completion) in an ADR or a dedicated section of CLAUDE.md / the eval README, explaining the design rationale and how to add a new prompt type to the correct pipeline.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural contract — a separate, findings-based scoring pipeline for code-review prompts distinct from the verdict-based pipeline for completion prompts — but names no ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design-doc update to capture this decision for future maintainers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify timeout, at least one retry with backoff, and a clear error-surfacing strategy (e.g., mark the case ERROR rather than PASS/FAIL) for LLM calls in the new code-review eval arm, and add at minimum a structured log entry for each call attempt/failure so CI artifacts are debuggable.
findings.finding: The new code-review eval arm in eval_solver.run_case makes live LLM calls, but the plan specifies no timeout, retry/backoff, or graceful-degradation behavior for those calls. A flaky or slow LLM provider will cause eval runs to fail non-deterministically, making it impossible to distinguish a real recall regression from an infrastructure blip. The plan is also silent on observability (logging, tracing, or CI artifact tagging) for these new failure points.
findings.location: Scope / eval_solver.py — new code-review arm; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the LLM call in the new arm with a configurable timeout (e.g., 30 s) and 1–2 exponential-backoff retries; on exhaustion, raise a distinct EvalInfraError that the harness catches and records as outcome='error' (not 'pass'/'fail'). Log prompt_id, attempt number, and error type at WARNING level so CI logs are searchable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (or AC1) what a verify-arm eval fixture must supply — either a `findings` list (which run_case serializes via review_kernel.verify_instructions) or a pre-serialized `instructions` string — so the verify arm cannot silently invoke the prompt with an empty/missing context.
findings.finding: The plan confidently asserts 'verify returns {verifications:[...]}' implying code-review-verify can be individually invoked in an eval run_case arm, but the actual code-review-verify prompt requires a pre-assembled `instructions` input built by the deterministic `code_review_verify_inputs` step (which serializes a merged findings list via review_kernel.verify_instructions). The plan provides no specification of how eval fixtures for the verify arm supply this required input, leaving the verify arm's implementation underspecified.
findings.location: ## What — OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT section; AC1 ('run_case executes code-review prompt ids (base, verify, overlays) returning the prompt's native structured output'); src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml lines 122-136
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan: 'A verify-arm fixture carries a `findings` list; run_case assembles the `instructions` string via review_kernel.verify_instructions(list(enumerate(findings))) before invoking the prompt, mirroring the code_review_verify_inputs deterministic step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the exact structured-output shape the code-review-verify arm should return: the prompt declares `outputs: verification` (single per-finding dict) not a `{verifications:[...]}` list — confirm whether the eval arm invokes verify once per finding (returning one verification dict) or assembles a list, and update the OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan states the code-review verify prompt returns `{verifications:[...]}` as its native structured output, but code-review-verify.md declares `outputs: verification` (the kernel's gate-agnostic per-finding verification contract, not a top-level verifications list). The workflow bus assembles a flat `verifications` list from individual per-finding outputs, but that is a workflow-level shape, not the direct prompt output. A run_case arm that expects `{verifications:[...]}` from a single prompt invocation will receive a differently shaped object.
findings.location: What section / OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT paragraph: 'verify returns {verifications:[...]}'; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md front-matter `outputs: verification`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific function/module the new run_case code-review arm will call for each prompt type (base, overlay, verify) — e.g. a new `run_single_reviewer(prompt_id, diff_text, ...)` in code_review/ — so the arm has a clear, existing or to-be-created entry point rather than needing to navigate the full workflow scaffold.
findings.finding: The plan names 'overlay ids' as `run_case` targets without specifying how an overlay prompt_id maps to its executable op. Unlike completion-verifier/ticket-quality/spec-alignment which have dedicated agentic ops in `operations.py`/`completion.py`/`spec_scan.py`, the overlay prompts (e.g. `code-review-tests`, `code-review-security`) are not top-level ops — they run as steps inside the workflow gate (code-review.yaml). The plan does not name which function/module the new run_case overlay arm will call, leaving the implementation path ambiguous.
findings.location: What section / Harness prerequisite paragraph: 'Add a run_case arm'; Scope section: 'eval_solver.py (new code-review arm)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism for code-review-verify gating scorers: how a recall case identifies the target-finding index within the `{verifications:[...]}` output, and how the scorer extracts and thresholds that specific entry's validity (e.g. a new `code_review_verify_recall` scorer that takes a `target_index` case field and checks `verifications[target_index].validity >= threshold`).
findings.finding: The plan specifies findings-presence/absence scorers for the base/overlay arms but leaves the code-review-verify gating scorer mechanism ambiguous. The verify prompt (code-review-verify) returns `{verifications:[...]}` — a flat list of per-finding validity records keyed by 0-based index — not a `{findings:[...]}` shape. A 'recall' case for code-review-verify must assert that a specific target finding's validity score in the verifications list clears a threshold, not merely that the list is non-empty. The plan does not describe how the gating scorer identifies the target-finding index within a multi-finding verifications output, creating a realistic risk of a trivially-satisfied or vacuous scorer for the verify spec.
findings.location: What section / OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT paragraph; AC line: '>=1 code_review_* gating scorer registered that scores on findings presence/absence'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `target_index` field to verify-recall dataset cases (the 0-based index of the seeded defect in the merged findings list). Register a new scorer `code_review_verify_recall` that extracts `out['verifications'][target_index]` and checks its validity >= 0.5 (using the existing `_validity()` helper). The no-fire analogue checks validity < 0.5 for the target index. This is the exact pattern `_discriminates_true_from_false` uses but applied to the right sub-item in a list-shaped output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `code-review` arm in `eval_solver.run_case` that handles prompt ids matching `code-review-base`, `code-review-verify`, and the 11 overlay ids — feeding a diff fixture (from `case['diff_text']` + `case['files']`) and returning the prompt's native structured output (`{findings:[...]}` for base/overlays, the verifier's per-finding verification object for verify).
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case has no execution arm for any code-review prompt id; it raises ValueError for 'code-review-base', 'code-review-verify', and all overlay ids — the harness prerequisite the plan identifies is still unimplemented.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py, run_case() lines 136-171 (the if/elif chain ends at 'spec-alignment'; no code-review branch exists)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an elif branch after the 'spec-alignment' arm: detect prompt_id starting with 'code-review-' or in registry.OVERLAY_IDS; use case_store to write fixture files; call the relevant prompting op with diff_text from case.get('diff_text') and return the structured output directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Register at least `code_review_recall` and `code_review_no_fire` scorers in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` that gate strictly on `findings` presence/absence (never on a `verdict` key), so code-review eval specs have named, contracted scorers that validate under `strict=True`.
findings.finding: eval_scorers.REGISTRY contains no code_review_* scorer entries; the plan requires at least one findings-based code-review gating scorer but none is registered, meaning any code-review eval spec would fail validate_eval_spec(strict=True) with 'not a registered scorer'.
findings.suggested_fix: Add two functions _code_review_recall and _code_review_no_fire that check `bool(out.get('findings'))` directly (no verdict check), and register them as 'code_review_recall' / 'code_review_no_fire' in REGISTRY.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve and document the exact dict shape `run_case` returns for `code-review-verify` (either the kernel's `{verifications: [...]}` wrapper or a single per-finding verification object), and pin that shape in the new scorer(s) so the contract is unambiguous before any spec is authored.
findings.finding: The plan's OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT states 'verify returns {verifications:[...]}', but the actual code-review-verify prompt uses output_schema 'verification' (the kernel's single-object-per-finding contract per code-review.yaml line 132) — the run_case arm's expected return shape for the verify prompt is contradicted by the live schema, leaving the arm's output contract ambiguous.
findings.location: Plan section 'OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT'; cross-referenced with src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml line 132 and src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md (frontmatter: `outputs: verification`)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly whether the BLOCK/block vocabulary extension modifies the shared `_fired`/`FIRE_EXPECTS` (with a guard that it only applies to code-review full-gate cases) or is isolated to a new `code_review_full_gate_recall` scorer that checks `verdict in {'BLOCK'}` directly — and document the decision co-located with `_fired` in eval_scorers.py.
findings.finding: The conditional extension of `_fired` / `FIRE_EXPECTS` to handle BLOCK verdicts has no specified placement: the plan says 'if and only if a case exercises the full-gate verdict, extend _fired to also match verdict==BLOCK and add block to FIRE_EXPECTS' but does not specify whether this lands in eval_scorers.py (modifying the shared _fired used by all scorers) or in a new code-review-specific scorer, creating an ambiguity that risks breaking existing plan-review/completion-verifier scorers.
findings.location: Plan section 'OUTPUT-SHAPE CONTRACT' (conditional BLOCK extension); src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py _fired() lines 73-79

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the nit-framing experiment eval case concretely: specify the target prompt_id (e.g. `code-review-base`), a `diff_text` fixture that presents a nit-level style issue alongside a genuine defect, the `expect` value for each (pass for the nit, finding for the defect), and which scorer gates the distinction — then commit it as a case in the corresponding `*.eval.yaml`.
findings.finding: The nit-framing experiment eval case (AC-5) is referenced as 'encoded as a committed eval case (fixture + scorer assertion)' but is entirely absent from the codebase — no fixture, no scorer assertion, and no specification of which prompt_id it targets or what its expect/diff_text payload should contain.
findings.location: Plan AC-5; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (no nit-framing file found); src/rebar/llm/evals/ (no nit-framing reference)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
